item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't understand the ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A great read, the monster of the week/day format worked really well, lots of funky skips on display as well.

item.title: A real tour de force...
item.content:encoded:
… in both the French and English meanings of that phrase :-)

One thing I don't get: SCP 316 at the beginning. Don't see how a 914-modified lantern projects you into in alternate reality, esp. one that's not dull. And 316-D doesn't seem to make sense either…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, nice!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those are the only two I can find, and I've been through this about three times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are there more that are outdated, or just those two?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, hell. Entry 11 doesn't make much sense now that SCP-179 has changed. For the sake of completeness, I've dug it up, and found the old 179 on the Wayback Machine here.

E: Same with the link to SCP-644 in Entry 26. Thank god Wayback Machine crawls our site regularly. Old SCP-644 here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Internet Archive has it: https://web.archive.org/web/20130301132700/http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/scp-179

I can see why it was replaced, it's just a thing that eats people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yup.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ha, it's alright, I think the wondering and guessing is a lot of the reason people like this tale.

As far as the general idea, it's not really important exactly where or when the group was sent, or PRECISELY what it was that happened there. The idea I was trying to explore was a basic apocalypse premise, but instead of one, repeatedly scary thing, just sort of… imagine what a crapton of SCiPs scattered around a dead world would do, and what it would be like to wander that world as a normal human being. Granted, the concession of "in-depth knowledge of SCiPs" was made to allow the story to progress past the first couple chapters, but these guys are all non-anomalous. It's more about the growing dread of realizing just how bad things can go if the Foundation fails than the specific history of the wrecked world. I was looking more at dimensional travel than time travel, really, but Xyank's involvement and 1780 were one of the only ways I could think to wrap the story up without the predictable "and then he dies" scene.

By the by, good job guessing, and well done making the connection between EC and… well, what it actually means. I always figured that it would make sense for those folks to be able to interact with the general Foundation populace without being immediately recognized, and this was a way to implement that.

Thanks for reading! This story took a looooong time to write, and I almost botched it entirely with my original idea to hide its parts in other articles. I'm glad it's still something folks enjoy reading- if I can get inspired, I'll see if I can't weave together another tale of its caliber.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this tale. Nice "wandering in a post-apoc alternate dimension" tone going on, although…well, I guess I can sort of see how a 914-ified 316 would have had that effect. Seems thematically fitting if nothing else. I liked how for the first half or so, the anomalies were linear — the first few were all Safes, then Euclids, then Keters; it was a nice "rising action"-y touch, though I'm not sure if it was intentional because you dropped it later on.

The end was interesting (in a good way). I'm not 100% sure what happened, but I have the general idea. I'm tentatively going to guess that

+ show block
– hide block

Piedmont and the others somehow ended up in a world where something slipped up on 231-7's containment and all hell broke loose, and in the end he's still in that alternate world, but he's been sent back in time within that timeline to stop the apocalypse. Or something. There are still a few details that I'm not quite sure how they fit in (for instance, what exactly 231 has to do with the ability to leave 1780), but overall it makes enough sense.

All of that being said, forgive me if my other main thought right now is "Oooh, a puzzle tale!" XD I know they're linked on the page, but still, better safe than spoilery, so here's what I recognized:

+ show block
– hide block

165, 919, 312, 447 (though not until the next log, so maybe it doesn't count), 176, 303, 732, 239, 298, and then obviously 1780 and 231 were stated outright. Of the ones I didn't get, I did recognize about half of them upon "peeking". It also hit me pretty quickly what EC really stood for as soon as he mentioned that it wasn't actually esoteric containment and implied that his actual job involved knowing a lot of details about a lot of SCPs. I feel all smart now. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think so. If there are, nobody approached me about the idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are there any audio adaptations of this? This would make for an excellent audio adaptation in the right hands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It used to be the thing that sorta drained people. All skeletal like, but it swelled up after eating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm… I'm reading up on all the scips I wasn't familiar with, and it seems 179 was replaced with something else earlier this year, so the 11th entry does not make any sense to me. Anyone recall what that one was before/if any of the others have also been changed since this tale was first written up?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shit! Gentlemen, you've caught me- those are both legitimate errors. Consider them both corrected.

… well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It just might be… Probably best to confirm that one before changing it, though. Good catch.

I noticed the log numbers don't seem correct after a point. 20 is listed twice. If it's supposed to be a split log like 24, it doesn't read that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The 2nd chapter concludes with "Night one." A typo, methinks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mind = blown

Man, that was a good read. Didn't run into too many I wasn't familiar with, but I'm going to start looking at the ones I didn't recognize to help contextualize everything a little better.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I'd completed it offline and posted it all at once, it might have earned more upvotes. I'm happy with it as is- it was a featured article and that's as much as I'd really ask for. It'll earn upvotes when it strikes fancies, but the chapters were released over a really long time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an EXCELENT tale; it's creepy, it's bleak and it's really interesting and fun to read. I didn't get many of the references mainly because of not having read many well known scp's yet, but the mystic is incredible nonetheless. I wonder why it has such a relatively low level of upvotes, especially having in mind that many other productions with the same outstanding level of quality are largely over 200 or 300 at the moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait… so, you choose the SCP's that you included in the story based on the game? Huh. I already enjoyed the story quite a bit, and that additional… level of difficulty, I suppose, makes me appreciate it all the more. Great work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, don't sweat it, Qwag.

Like Ihpkmn said, there's some red text in the story itself that can shed some insight on each chapter. Look around and you'll see it. I don't expect everyone to identify the skips from memory. Heck, something that crazy should be a game.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What don't you get about it? If you're having trouble understanding it, I suggest clicking on the red text.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… I don't get it…

Edit: I mean, I don't get what some of the SCPs are. Probably because I haven't read all of them. In fact, just ignore this post. Stupid newbie. Please ignore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can't remember the last time I spent this much time reading SCPs. Introduced me to a lot of great old stuff I'd never seen or heard of before, 919 in particular. I'm a little confused by the ending, but I'm also amused that the 447-pocalypse was enacted by a fucking jet-propelled hand dryer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's definitely a great read through, in my opinion. Foundation failing, anomalous subjects running free, abandoned facilities and everything all wrap together to make an awe inspiring tale!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Over a year in the making, Incursion is probably one of my favorite tales on the entire site. If you haven't read this yet, please, take some time out of your day to look at this masterpiece.

Lowell, you should be proud of what you have done here. Very, very proud. I've already given my upvote, and I don't regret it in the slightest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
omgomgomg you featured 1780 you said you were going to and now it's coming I can't wait eeeeeee ^_^

Can't wait to see how you handle it. If you need anything, drop me a line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I totally agree. I want more. This is very good.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…this is probably one of the best tales I've read in recent memory. Just… wow.

+1. More people need to read this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last link ("24 Cont.") should have linked to SCP-890. There is no SCP-0890.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If it was a different language, reference would have been made to that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It sounds like it's an alternate universe, so that detail could be fuzzed over. Alternate UK could have gun shops around, could have an entirely different language (assuming that the map wasn't Welsh or Gaelic, as that could still be translated or construed normal from the English characters), could have no ties whatsoever with the surrounding world. The story is still enjoyable. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There aren't any gun shops in the UK.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reads like a Foundation/Silent Hill crossover plot from the perspective of the man character.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ [SPOILER REDACTED]
- hide this content

Each chapter number is linked to the SCP referenced in that chapter. Should help make things clearer, but it DOES sort of spoil the mystique.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I appreciate the fact that these are now all in one page, though personally I'm still not quite sure what in the sweet hell is going on in like… Half of them, and I hope there's a good summation or post-action report when it's all said and done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tags would indicate a report that could be searched. If this reaches that point, I'll start adding tags. As of yet, I don't want tags on these pages.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
NO.

Absolutely not. If you are posting tales, post them as a tale. You do NOT have permission to hide tales in other peoples articles. Please remove any links you may have added in articles that are not your own.

Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are you responding to something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, or at least not yet. That'll get explained later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should this have tags?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was pointed out that there shouldn't be D-class tests on 914 outputs in general. Thanks for the observation, I'm fixing it now. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't say I like your "insert hidden links into other articles" thing, but the tales are good.

Also, there is an inconsistency - "1" says that the 914-processed replica was activated by D-Class, while 914 experiment logs say that it was done by Dr. F█████

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or you can ruin the fun and progress the HTML. Choice is yours! Read or play!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mhm. You'll have to find it, though. If you're having trouble, send me a PM and I'll tell you how to know which SCPs to check.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i assume there is going to be a 2?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting choice of SCP. Cleaned a spot of grammar, if that's ok. Looking forward to more of this.
